projectDependencyRules.description: automation modules must not depend on controller modules
projectDependencyRules.suggestion: depend on a controller-neutral contract module; controller implementations may depend inward on that contract

projectDependencyRules.description: the control shell must not depend on controller implementations
projectDependencyRules.suggestion: resolve controllers through the plugin/registry contract instead of a project dependency

projectDependencyRules.description: automation API must remain independent from the Android app
projectDependencyRules.suggestion: keep contracts in :automation:api and place Android adapters in :app

projectDependencyRules.description: automation API must not depend on Android hidden APIs
projectDependencyRules.suggestion: place device and Android implementation details in a runtime module

projectDependencyRules.description: remote runtime must not depend on the application or MAA implementation
projectDependencyRules.suggestion: put compatibility composition in the app legacy binder

projectDependencyRules.description: generic IPC contracts must not depend on app AIDL or MAA types
projectDependencyRules.suggestion: keep IPC parcelables and interfaces controller-neutral

importRules.description: remote runtime must not import MAA or JNA implementation types
importRules.suggestion: compose MAA compatibility only in app/src/main/.../remote/legacy

importRules.description: generic automation IPC must not import legacy MAA or app service contracts
automation API must be pure Kotlin without Android, Compose, MAA, JNA, Root, or Shizuku imports
importRules.suggestion: move platform/controller implementation code to an adapter or runtime module

importRules.description: app/src/main must not gain new MAA/controller implementation imports (Phase 0 ratchet)
importRules.suggestion: move the dependency behind a controller-neutral API; Phase 6 removes this baseline

importRules.description: hostOnly sources must not import the MAA controller implementation
